data.description: Replace current dark glass aesthetic (rgba black) with unified white Google Maps style. Create CSS custom properties (tokens) for bg:#fff, border:#e0e0e0, shadow, radius, font. This is a prerequisite for all other UI tasks. All existing elements (search bar, toggle, panels) get reskinned.
data.title: Google Maps UI design system

data.body:
VISUAL SPEC FROM MOCKUPS (docs/mockups/):

Background: pure black (#000 or very close). Globe is the main visual — UI is chrome on top.
App title: 'GOOGLE MARS' (or 'Red Planet') — centered top, white, small caps, lightweight.

Card/panel aesthetic: off-white/light gray fill (not pure white), rounded corners (~12px), subtle 1px border, soft drop shadow. NOT transparent dark glass.

Design tokens to establish:
  --surface: #f8f9fa (card bg)
  --border: #e0e0e0
  --shadow: 0 2px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.3)
  --radius: 12px
  --text: #1a1a1a
  --text-secondary: #5f6368
  --font: system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif

All interactive elements (buttons, icons, search rows, checkboxes) need hover states — cursor pointer, subtle bg tint (#00000008 or similar).

Mockups show consistent card language across search panel, feature info card, and layers panel — same radius, same bg, same border. Must be unified via tokens.

Additional: App title bar. 'GOOGLE MARS' (placeholder — final name TBD, probably 'Red Planet') — white, centered top, small caps, lightweight font. This is a static element but needs to be part of the design system pass.
CLARIFICATION: App name is 'RED PLANET' — full caps, white text, centered top. Not 'Google Mars'.
